title: A Fully Ab-Initio Spin-Lattice Dynamics Framework for Magnetic Materials
authors: Xianxi Zhang, Hongyu Yu, Liangliang Hong, Hongjun Xiang
abstract: Coupled spin-lattice dynamics (SLD) underlie a wide range of magnetic phenomena, yet a unified first-principles framework that propagates both degrees of freedom without empirical parameterization has remained elusive. We present a fully ab initio SLD approach integrated into VASP, in which interatomic forces and effective magnetic fields are obtained at each time step from self-consistent constrained-moment density-functional calculations. The method is validated on four materials spanning ferromagnetic, non-collinear, and geometrically frustrated orders, recovering the correct magnetic ground state in every case from random initial conditions. SLD trajectories also provide physically correlated training data for magnetic machine-learning potentials, as demonstrated for BiFeO$_3$ by a reduction of up to approximately one order of magnitude in energy MAE over training on randomized spin configurations. This framework opens a practical first-principles route to finite-temperature spin-lattice coupled phenomena in magnetic materials.
summary: This paper introduces a new first-principles method to simulate the simultaneous motion of atoms and spins in magnetic materials without needing pre-fitted parameters. It successfully predicts complex magnetic phases and shows that the data generated by this method can significantly improve the accuracy of machine-learning models for magnetism.
main_problem: The lack of a unified, first-principles framework capable of propagating both spin and lattice degrees of freedom simultaneously without relying on empirical parameterization.
main_result: The development of a fully ab initio spin-lattice dynamics (SLD) framework in VASP that accurately recovers diverse magnetic ground states and provides highly efficient, physically correlated training data for machine-learning potentials.
method: A self-consistent dynamical loop integrated into VASP using constrained-moment density functional theory to obtain interatomic forces and effective magnetic fields at each time step.
model_or_system: A coupled spin-lattice dynamics model where the lattice follows Newtonian equations of motion and spins follow the stochastic Landau-Lifshitz-Gilbert equation, applied to magnetic materials such as CrI3, bcc Fe, Cd2Os2O7, MgCr2O4, and BiFeO3.
key_observables: Interatomic forces, effective magnetic fields, magnetic order parameters (e.g., AIAO order parameter), magnetization, and lattice parameters (c/a ratio).
important_parameters: Time step (up to 1 fs), gyromagnetic ratio, Gilbert damping parameter, and the global penalty strength for magnetic constraints.
main_assumptions: The spin-adiabatic approximation (instantaneous adaptation of moment magnitudes) and the atomic-moment approximation (local magnetic moments are treated as discrete variables).
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the dynamical loop of the SLD framework; Figure 3 benchmarks SpinGNN models using SLD-derived data against randomized-spin datasets across different training budgets and sampling strategies.
paper_structure: The paper identifies the gap in current dynamical modeling, presents the new self-consistent VASP implementation, validates the physics across diverse magnetic orders, and demonstrates the utility of the resulting data for machine learning.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Schematic of the ab initio SLD dynamical loop im- plemented in VASP. At each time step, a constrained-moment DFT calculation yields both the interatomic forces {Fi} and the effective magnetic fields {Bi}, which are used to advance ionic positions via velocity-Verlet and spin directions via the SIB integrator, respectively.

FIG. 2. Magnetic ground-state validation across four representative systems. (a) Monolayer CrI3: convergence of the out- of-plane magnetization from a random initial state to the ferromagnetic ground state. (b) Bcc Fe: convergence of the total magnetization to the collinear ferromagnetic state at 300 K. (c) Cd2Os2O7: final spin configuration showing the AIAO ar- rangement. (d) Cd2Os2O7: time evolution of the AIAO order parameter ms converging to ≈0.9. (e) MgCr2O4: spontaneous tetragonal distortion (c/a ratio) accompanying the onset of collinear antiferromagnetic order during annealing from 50 K to 5 K. (f) MgCr2O4: final spin configuration.

FIG. 3. Training-data benchmark for SLD-oriented SpinGNN models in BiFeO3. All mean absolute errors are evaluated on an independent SLD test trajectory. Panels (a)–(c) show the fixed-count comparison between the SCF reference and SLD- derived datasets for energy, forces, and spin forces, respectively. Panels (d)–(f) show the fixed-budget comparison among the SCF reference, the SLD-derived dataset generated at the same SCF-equivalent budget, and its strided counterpart used to reduce temporal correlation.
